header.servicer: Chase Home Finance (transferring to Rocket Mortgage effective May 15, 2026)

sections.heading: Action Items and Urgent Tasks
sections.body: This section lists all current action items and urgent tasks for 4418 Cedar Ridge Dr, Riverside, CA 92504 (Case No. 2026-FC-03217), as drawn from both servicer communications and the latest court order. Each action item includes its priority, deadline (if applicable), and explicit assignment to an owner only where the source document or communication establishes responsibility. Where owner is not stated in the evidence, the field is omitted. Items are cross-referenced to authoritative evidence, and deadlines are directly linked to their required actions.

blocks.title: Direct all correspondence to Rocket Mortgage
blocks.notes: Effective May 15, 2026, all loan servicing responsibilities transfer from Chase Home Finance to Rocket Mortgage. Any invoices submitted to Chase after this date will be rejected.
action_items: After May 15, 2026, send all future correspondence, billing, and status reports regarding the loan to Rocket Mortgage.

citations.claim: Direct all future correspondence, billing, and status reports regarding the loan to Rocket Mortgage after May 15, 2026.

spans.raw_text:
First, effective May 15, 2026, the servicing of this loan will transfer from
Chase Home Finance to Rocket Mortgage. Please direct all future correspondence,
billing, and status reports to Rocket Mortgage after that date. The new address
is 1050 Woodward Ave, Detroit, MI 48226 and the new contact is Marcus Alvarez
(malvarez@rocketmortgage.com). Any invoices submitted to Chase after May 15
will be bounced, so please route accordingly.

blocks.title: Confirm appearance at case management conference
blocks.notes: Conference set in attached court order; confirmation of appearance required.
action_items: Confirm your appearance at the case management conference scheduled for June 18, 2026.

citations.claim: Case management conference is set for June 18, 2026 (see attached court order). Please confirm your appearance.

spans.raw_text:
- Case management conference is set for June 18, 2026 (see attached court
order). Please confirm your appearance.

blocks.title: Review IRS party status for federal tax lien
blocks.notes: Review whether a newly filed federal tax lien requires the IRS to be named as a party defendant in the foreclosure complaint, due to statutory notice requirements under 28 U.S.C. § 2410. Advise accordingly.
action_items: Determine if the IRS must be joined as a party defendant based on new federal tax lien and statutory notice requirements.

citations.claim: Review whether the IRS needs to be named as a party defendant in the foreclosure complaint due to a newly filed federal tax lien and applicable statutory notice requirements. Advise accordingly.

spans.raw_text:
- We received a lien-title update showing a federal tax lien filed against the
  borrower by the IRS for $47,829.03. Please review whether the IRS needs to be
  named as a party defendant in the foreclosure complaint  our understanding
  is the federal statutory redemption period creates specific notice requirements
  under 28 U.S.C. a7 2410. Please advise.

blocks.title: Address Hernandez & Sons mechanic's lien before sale
blocks.notes: A mechanic's lien for $18,650.00 from Hernandez & Sons Construction is outstanding and flagged on title; must be resolved prior to property sale.
action_items: Resolve the mechanic's lien for $18,650.00 from Hernandez & Sons Construction prior to any sale.

citations.claim: Address the mechanic's lien for $18,650.00 from Hernandez & Sons Construction before any sale.

spans.raw_text:
- Also flagged on title: a mechanic's lien for $18,650.00 from Hernandez & Sons
  Construction for a bathroom/kitchen remodel. Needs to be addressed before
  any sale.

blocks.title: File proof of service on all named defendants, including IRS
blocks.notes: The court order requires filing proof of service, including on the IRS under 28 U.S.C. § 2410.
action_items: File proof of service for all named defendants, including the Internal Revenue Service.

citations.claim: Proof of service on all named defendants, including the Internal Revenue Service pursuant to 28 U.S.C. § 2410, must be filed no later than June 1, 2026.

spans.raw_text:
3. Proof of service on all named defendants, including the Internal
   Revenue Service pursuant to 28 U.S.C. § 2410, must be filed no later
   than June 1, 2026.

blocks.title: File case management statement addressing specified topics
blocks.notes: Per court order, Plaintiff shall file a Case Management Statement by June 8, 2026, ten days before the conference. The statement must address service, parties to be joined (including federal agencies), settlement status, trial readiness, and any pending motions.
action_items: File a Case Management Statement covering all topics specified in court order by June 8, 2026.

citations.claim: Plaintiff shall file a Case Management Statement no later than June 8, 2026 — ten (10) calendar days prior to the conference.

spans.raw_text:
2. Plaintiff shall file a Case Management Statement no later than
   June 8, 2026 — ten (10) calendar days prior to the conference. The
   statement must address: (a) service of process on all named defendants,
   (b) any defendants who must be joined including federal agencies,
   (c) status of settlement discussions, (d) trial readiness estimate,
   and (e) any pending motions.

blocks.title: Refresh BPO or valuation with updated payoff
blocks.notes: Updated payoff is $643,718.44 as of April 1, 2026. Any pending BPO or valuation work should use this figure.
action_items: Refresh any pending BPO or valuation work using the payoff figure $643,718.44 as of April 1, 2026.

citations.claim: Refresh any pending BPO or valuation work using the updated payoff amount of $643,718.44 as of April 1, 2026.

spans.raw_text:
- Updated payoff as of April 1, 2026: $643,718.44. Please refresh any pending
  BPO or valuation work with this figure.

blocks.title: Log borrower correspondence per protocol until counsel retained
blocks.notes: As borrower Linda M. Thompson has not retained counsel as of the latest communication, continue logging and handling all inbound borrower correspondence through Chase Home Finance per standard protocol until counsel is noted.
action_items: Log inbound correspondence from Thompson and handle through Chase per standard protocol until counsel is retained.

citations.claim: Log any inbound correspondence from Thompson per standard protocol and continue to handle through Chase Home Finance until the borrower retains counsel.

spans.raw_text:
- Borrower has NOT retained counsel as of today. Any inbound correspondence from
Thompson should still be logged per standard protocol but continue to be
handled through us until counsel is noted.

sections.heading: Upcoming Deadlines, Filings, and Hearings
sections.body:
This section outlines all known upcoming case deadlines, required filings, and scheduled hearings in chronological order. Key identification details are included per operator guidelines. All facts are cross-referenced to official court orders and corroborating email communications to present a unified and comprehensive timeline of required actions in this case.

Case Identification Details:
- Preparation Date: Not specified in evidence
- Court: Not stated in source but inferred as Riverside, CA (see jurisdiction)
- Judge: Not identified in available materials
- Case Number: 2026-FC-03217
- Parties' Counsel: All counsel of record (as per court order)

Please review all events and associated requirements below. Action items are prioritized by urgency and statutory compliance.

blocks.title: Proof of Service Deadline

fields.value: File proof of service on all named defendants, including the Internal Revenue Service pursuant to 28 U.S.C. § 2410.
Proof of service must be filed with the Court to establish proper notice to all parties.

action_items: Prepare and file proof of service on all named defendants, including the IRS, by June 1, 2026.

citations.claim: Proof of service on all named defendants, including the Internal Revenue Service pursuant to 28 U.S.C. § 2410, must be filed no later than June 1, 2026.

spans.raw_text:
3. Proof of service on all named defendants, including the Internal
   Revenue Service pursuant to 28 U.S.C. § 2410, must be filed no later
   than June 1, 2026.

blocks.title: Case Management Statement Filing

fields.value: Plaintiff must file a Case Management Statement addressing service of process, defendants to be joined, status of settlement discussions, trial readiness estimate, and any pending motions.
Comprehensive Case Management Statement must be filed covering all items required by court order.

action_items: Complete and file the Case Management Statement by June 8, 2026, addressing all enumerated requirements.

citations.claim: Plaintiff shall file a Case Management Statement no later than June 8, 2026, addressing process, joinder, status of settlement, trial readiness, and pending motions.

spans.raw_text:
2. Plaintiff shall file a Case Management Statement no later than
   June 8, 2026 — ten (10) calendar days prior to the conference. The
   statement must address: (a) service of process on all named defendants,
   (b) any defendants who must be joined including federal agencies,
   (c) status of settlement discussions, (d) trial readiness estimate,
   and (e) any pending motions.

blocks.title: Payoff Statement Lodgment

fields.value: Counsel for Plaintiff must produce a payoff statement current within thirty (30) days of the upcoming conference and lodge it with the Court.
The Court must receive a payoff statement (current within 30 days) from Plaintiff’s counsel by June 13, 2026.

action_items: Prepare and lodge a payoff statement, current within 30 days of the conference, with the Court by June 13, 2026.

citations.claim: Counsel for Plaintiff shall produce a payoff statement current within thirty (30) days of the conference and lodge it with the Court by June 13, 2026.

spans.raw_text:
4. Counsel for Plaintiff shall produce a payoff statement current within
   thirty (30) days of the conference and lodge it with the Court by
   June 13, 2026.

blocks.title: Case Management Conference

fields.value: All counsel of record must appear in person (telephonic appearance not permitted without good cause).
Counsel must attend the conference in Department 3; ensure all pre-conference filings are complete.

blocks.notes: Confirmed by court order and referenced in servicer counsel email.
action_items: Ensure all counsel of record are present in Department 3 at 9:00 AM on June 18, 2026. Telephonic appearance not allowed except for good cause.

citations.claim: A Case Management Conference is set for June 18, 2026, at 9:00 AM in Department 3. All counsel of record must appear in person; telephonic appearance is not permitted absent good cause.

spans.raw_text:
1. A Case Management Conference is set for June 18, 2026, at 9:00 AM in
   Department 3. All counsel of record must appear in person; telephonic
   appearance is not permitted absent good cause.

citations.claim: Case management conference is set for June 18, 2026 (see attached court order). Please confirm your appearance.

spans.raw_text:
- Case management conference is set for June 18, 2026 (see attached court
  order). Please confirm your appearance.

citations.claim: All cited deadlines, actions, and hearing appearance obligations are sourced from the case's official court order and corroborated by counsel correspondence.

spans.raw_text:
1. A Case Management Conference is set for June 18, 2026, at 9:00 AM in
   Department 3. All counsel of record must appear in person; telephonic
   appearance is not permitted absent good cause.
2. Plaintiff shall file a Case Management Statement no later than
   June 8, 2026 — ten (10) calendar days prior to the conference. The
   statement must address: (a) service of process on all named defendants,
   (b) any defendants who must be joined including federal agencies,
   (c) status of settlement discussions, (d) trial readiness estimate,
   and (e) any pending motions.
3. Proof of service on all named defendants, including the Internal
   Revenue Service pursuant to 28 U.S.C. § 2410, must be filed no later
   than June 1, 2026.
4. Counsel for Plaintiff shall produce a payoff statement current within
   thirty (30) days of the conference and lodge it with the Court by
   June 13, 2026.

- Case management conference is set for June 18, 2026 (see attached court
  order). Please confirm your appearance.

sections.body: This section documents the recent servicing transfer for the subject loan and provides the current status regarding borrower's counsel retention.

action_items: Update all communications and billing processes to use Rocket Mortgage as servicer after May 15, 2026.

citations.claim: Servicing of this loan will transfer from Chase Home Finance to Rocket Mortgage effective May 15, 2026.

sections.heading: Title Concerns Relevant to Case Posture
sections.body: Significant title concerns affecting this foreclosure include active liens, delinquent property taxes, a recent federal tax lien, and a mechanic's lien that require immediate attention. Each of these could impact the foreclosure timeline and party-naming decisions.

blocks.title: Delinquent Property Taxes – Second Installment Due
blocks.notes: Outstanding property taxes must be addressed to avoid tax sale risk.
action_items: Confirm payment of second installment to avoid tax sale or priority issues.

citations.claim: Outstanding 2025 property taxes—second installment of $11,342.00 due April 10, 2026, remains unpaid.

spans.raw_text:
1. Property taxes and assessments for the year 2025, second
   insta11ment due Apri1 10, 2026.
   Amount: $11,342.00 (second insta11ment)
   APN: 234-056-007-3

blocks.title: Active Federal Tax Lien Filed by IRS
blocks.notes: Federal tax lien introduces potential party-naming and statutory notice requirements under 28 U.S.C. § 2410. IRS may need to be named as a defendant; failure to comply could delay or invalidate foreclosure.
action_items: Review compliance with federal notice requirements; ensure IRS properly named in complaint.

citations.claim: IRS filed federal tax lien for $47,829.03 (tax years 2022-2023) against the borrower, recorded November 4, 2025, as Instrument No. 2025-0114872.

spans.raw_text:
